dialogos.texto_en: (After Uesugi-san went to Tokyo\n and my college life began%p-2;――%p;)
(The following months went by\n in the blink of an eye.)
(Recently, I finally feel like\n I'm actually a college student, and it's sinking in.)
(Like, in moments like these...)
Hmm...mmm...
Looks like you're struggling with your report.
Itsukii!\nI'm about to hit my limit.
*sigh*\nAt first, I thought it would be like\nhigh school homework, you know?
As I was doing it, I started to lose track\nof what I was even writing about.
Besides, the focus is different\nbetween high school assignments and college reports.
There's only one more week until the deadline.\nI want at least a C...\nI have to work hard here.
...Ah.\nYotsuba, isn't it time already?
Oh, right!\nThanks, Itsuki!
Good luck today.
Alright, I have to get ready quickly.
Today is an important online class day\nwith Uesugi-san, after all.
(This is an important time to fulfill our promise.)
Hmm,\nI didn't understand some parts of the lesson...
Oh, I know!\nMaybe I'll ask Uesugi-san for his opinion\non the theme of the report I'm working on right now.
But, there's not enough time for everything...
Hmm...\nI have to become better at studying.\nI can't rely on Uesugi-san for everything.
(I'm still bad at studying, but once I'm done with it,\n here comes my favorite time of the day.)
What should we talk about after class?
Like, if the new clothes I bought were\nunpopular with everyone, or if the beef bowl\nat the school cafeteria was delicious!
Hmm?\nSomehow, my topics don't seem very interesting.
Let's try to have a conversation like lovers...
I'm not quite ready for that yet.\nAlright, I'll leave today's topic to Uesugi-san.
(Small, trivial everyday topics,\n like when we were in high school.\n Although insignificant, it was a fun conversation.)
(Thanks to that, even though we're apart,\n I feel like he was sitting right next to me.)
...But, I still want to see you.
Futaro...
...Hm?\nA text?
WHAAA?!\nU-U-Uesugi-san? Why?!
What?! When did that happen?!
So, just wait a moment.]
U-um, when did you start listening in?
Ah, never mind! You don't have to say anything!\nMore importantly, just%p-2;――
Please take care of me today!
